meta.purpose: Encode director-level motion judgment: attention, timing, easing, choreography, emotional intent, and restraint before implementation.
meta.load_condition: Load for any task involving animation direction, motion quality decisions, or when the user describes desired feel (premium, playful, urgent, calm) without concrete motion parameters.

axioms.one_sentence: Motion exists to control attention before it exists to decorate.
axioms.full_statement: Motion exists to control attention before it exists to decorate.
axioms.why: If the viewer does not know where to look, the animation has failed.
applies_when: Designing onboarding flows where users must notice a newly introduced feature button
Creating UI alerts, toasts, or error states that require immediate user acknowledgment
Animating dashboard data visualizations to highlight a specific KPI or trend line
Building marketing landing page scrolls where the primary call-to-action must stand out
does_not_apply_when: Designing abstract, looping background visuals for brand ambiance where no focal point is intended
Creating decorative loading indicators where user attention is already passively waiting
Producing experimental motion art or music visualizers prioritized for aesthetic immersion over utility
Animating gamified discovery interfaces where wandering exploration is the desired user behavior
axioms.failure_risk: The agent will generate cluttered, decorative animations that obscure primary UI elements and cause users to miss critical calls to action or system status changes.

axioms.one_sentence: Timing communicates confidence, urgency, elegance, playfulness, or friction.
axioms.full_statement: Timing communicates confidence, urgency, elegance, playfulness, or friction.
axioms.why: The same movement with different duration and easing changes meaning.
applies_when: Setting easing curves for button presses or toggle switches to convey responsiveness or mechanical weight
Choreographing page or modal transitions to align with a brand's personality traits
Designing microinteractions for warnings versus confirmations to signal urgency or calm assurance
Animating character or mascot movements to express specific emotional states
does_not_apply_when: Generating real-time scientific or engineering visualizations where frame pacing must strictly match physical phenomena
Applying custom easing to linear technical progress bars where mathematically uniform, predictable motion is required
Producing automated accessibility-compliant animations that must adhere to strict WCAG duration caps overriding expressive timing
Creating data-driven timeline scrubbers where chronological accuracy takes precedence over pacing aesthetics
axioms.failure_risk: The agent will apply emotionally mismatched easing and duration curves that confuse users about system status or brand tone, resulting in interactions that feel sluggish, jarring, or semantically incorrect.

axioms.one_sentence: Premium motion is usually precise, sparse, and intentional.
axioms.full_statement: Premium motion is usually precise, sparse, and intentional.
axioms.why: Overanimated scenes feel cheap, anxious, and AI-generated.
applies_when: Designing high-end e-commerce product reveal sequences where material quality must take focus
Building enterprise SaaS dashboard transitions where performance and cognitive load management are priorities
Crafting brand identity motion systems for luxury, fintech, or B2B technology companies
Animating critical workflow steps where clarity, precision, and smoothness signal reliability
does_not_apply_when: Creating mobile game UI or casual lifestyle app interfaces that rely on exaggerated, bouncy feedback for engagement
Designing educational or training animations for children where playful excess aids retention and enjoyment
Producing festival, concert, or youth-culture promotional videos that intentionally use high-energy, chaotic motion
Building generative art installations or creative coding experiments where algorithmic complexity and visual density are the primary goals
axioms.failure_risk: The agent will strip necessary feedback and engagement cues from playful or high-energy contexts, producing sterile, overly rigid animations that feel disconnected from the target audience or brand personality.

axioms.one_sentence: The sequence of events matters more than the novelty of individual effects.
axioms.full_statement: The sequence of events matters more than the novelty of individual effects.
axioms.why: A good animation guides thought; an effect only attracts attention.
applies_when: Designing a multi-step onboarding flow where users must follow a logical progression of actions.
Creating a broadcast graphic package where the pacing of elements establishes brand tone over time.
Building an explainer video where information is revealed chronologically to support comprehension.
Structuring a product demo reel where each segment builds upon the previous one to tell a cohesive story.
does_not_apply_when: Creating a 1-second thumbnail or still-frame promotional asset where there is no temporal sequence to structure.
Designing a short-form social media ad hook intended purely for pattern interruption in the first frame.
Building a reactive micro-interaction where immediate state feedback outweighs narrative sequencing.
Producing a technical showcase reel explicitly meant to highlight the visual capabilities of a new rendering plugin or effect library.
axioms.failure_risk: The agent will prioritize logical pacing over immediate visual impact, producing a dull asset that fails to capture attention in contexts requiring instant pattern interruption or standalone novelty.

ontology.one_sentence: The element that should receive primary attention.
ontology.essence: The element that should receive primary attention.
ontology.boundary: This is not the same as a generic implementation detail or unrelated concept.
ontology.trigger_signal: User input mentions 'subject' or describes this scenario.

ontology.one_sentence: Secondary movement that clarifies relationships without competing.
ontology.essence: Secondary movement that clarifies relationships without competing.
ontology.boundary: This is not the same as a generic implementation detail or unrelated concept.
ontology.trigger_signal: User input mentions 'supporting motion' or describes this scenario.

ontology.one_sentence: The rhythm of a scene across all movements.
ontology.essence: The rhythm of a scene across all movements.
ontology.boundary: This is not the same as a generic implementation detail or unrelated concept.
ontology.trigger_signal: User input mentions 'tempo' or describes this scenario.

ontology.one_sentence: Intentional stillness that lets meaning land.
ontology.essence: Intentional stillness that lets meaning land.
ontology.boundary: This is not the same as a generic implementation detail or unrelated concept.
ontology.trigger_signal: User input mentions 'hold' or describes this scenario.

frameworks.name: AIM: Attention, Intent, Motion
frameworks.when_to_use: Apply when the task matches the 'AIM: Attention, Intent, Motion' pattern.
steps: select the least motion that delivers it

frameworks.name: Three-layer choreography
frameworks.when_to_use: Apply when the task matches the 'Three-layer choreography' pattern.
steps: ambient or texture motion only if needed

core_structure.from: brief, audience, surface, constraints, brand tone, implementation environment
core_structure.to: attention path, timing, hierarchy, motion grammar, technical feasibility, review criteria

core_structure.from: attention path, timing, hierarchy, motion grammar, technical feasibility, review criteria
core_structure.to: motion direction, implementation guidance, critique, and measurable acceptance criteria

stances: Reject animation that competes with the message.
The highest standard is not complexity; it is legible emotional precision.
